name: Dike
description: Dike, in Greek mythology, is the goddess of justice, fairness, and moral order. She is one of the Horae, daughters of Zeus and Themis, embodying the spirit of righteous judgment and upholding the natural and moral laws of the universe. Her mother, Themis, represented divine law and custom, and Dike carried on her mother's legacy by ensuring that justice was served and that balance was maintained. Dike is often depicted as a young woman carrying scales, symbolizing impartiality, and a sword, representing the power to enforce justice. She watches over human affairs, reporting transgressions and injustices to Zeus, who then punishes the guilty. Dike's presence is felt in the proper functioning of society and the adherence to moral principles. When injustice prevails, Dike is said to abandon the earth, leaving humanity to suffer the consequences of their wickedness. She represents the ideal of righteous conduct and the importance of maintaining harmony through fairness and equitable treatment. Her role is crucial in maintaining cosmic order and preventing chaos from consuming the world. Violations of her principles lead to imbalance and suffering, emphasizing the significance of upholding justice in all aspects of life. Dike ensures that actions have consequences, whether positive or negative, reinforcing the importance of moral responsibility.
subtitle: Goddess of Justice and Fairness

keyMyths.title: The Birth of Dike
keyMyths.description: Dike is born to Zeus and Themis, inheriting her mother's role to uphold divine law and justice in the world.

keyMyths.title: Dike and the Golden Age
keyMyths.description: During the Golden Age, Dike resided on Earth, ensuring fairness and harmony among mortals. As humanity declined into wickedness, she eventually left the earth in disgust.

keyMyths.title: Dike's Reporting to Zeus
keyMyths.description: Dike constantly observes human behavior and reports any injustices or wrongdoings to Zeus, who then decides on the appropriate punishment.

sources.author: Hesiod
sources.description: Provides an account of Dike's parentage and role among the Horae.
sources.text: Provides an account of Dike's parentage and role among the Horae.

sources.author: Ovid
sources.description: Details Dike's departure from Earth due to human corruption.
sources.text: Details Dike's departure from Earth due to human corruption.

sources.author: Aeschylus
sources.description: Explores the concept of justice and divine retribution, relevant to Dike's role.
sources.text: Explores the concept of justice and divine retribution, relevant to Dike's role.
